_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed'; use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Fix issue #24: the composite GitHub Action ran `npm ci && npm run build` on every invocation, adding install/build latency and flakiness to every gated PR across every adopter repo. Ship a prebuilt, self-contained bundle (dist/index.js, deps inlined) committed to the repo, and run it directly from action.yml so the gate step starts in seconds with no runtime install/build. Add a CI dist-check job so the bundle cannot drift from source. Inputs/outputs and gate/review logic are unchanged.

validation_plan.reason: Type-check the source that feeds the bundle — the bundle can only be trusted if src/ still type-checks
tsc still produces dist/cli.js (the npm bin) and dist/test/ — bundling must not break the existing package build
Full suite (policy required_check) — proves no behavior regression in shape/review/CLI

validation_plan.command: npm run bundle && git diff --quiet -- dist/index.js
validation_plan.reason: The committed bundle must be byte-identical to a fresh rebuild — this is exactly what the dist-check CI job enforces
Confirms the bundled artifact parses the same CLI args as the old dist/cli.js and reaches receipt discovery — inputs/outputs unchanged

execution_evidence.command: npm run bundle && git diff --quiet -- dist/index.js
execution_evidence.output_ref: rebuild is byte-identical, no diff (dist-check passes)
args parsed, reached receipt discovery identically to dist/cli.js

result_summary: Added esbuild bundling (`npm run bundle` -> single self-contained dist/index.js with zod inlined; `npm run package` = build + bundle). action.yml now runs `node dist/index.js run ...` directly and no longer does `npm ci`/`npm run build` at runtime; the git base-ref fetch and all inputs/outputs are unchanged. Committed dist/index.js (un-ignored; tsc output stays ignored). Added a CI dist-check job that rebuilds the bundle and fails if it drifts from src/. Verified: tsc --noEmit clean, tsc build clean, 101/101 tests pass, rebuild is byte-identical to the committed bundle, and the bundled `run` command parses args identically to the old dist/cli.js. Gate/review logic, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG, and package version were not touched.
